skill.name: auth-handler
skill.description: Manage authentication, authorization, and user sessions. Use when dealing with login, sign-up, API protection, middleware, or user data fetching.
skill.summary: Guide secure NextAuth route protection, session handling, and user data access patterns.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as Ruby shell execution, but the lines are inline code references and a fenced TypeScript example. The weak-crypto and network-reconnaissance alerts are also false positives on descriptive authentication text. No evidence of executable code, prompt injection, exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Authentication changes can break access control or expose user data. This skill gives direct guidance for route wrappers, sessions, and user lookups.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the preferred wrappers for standard, super admin, and cron-protected API routes.
Directs client components to use the project user hook for frontend data access.
Guides server-side authentication checks through the project auth import.
Documents where core auth configuration, schema, middleware, and helper files are located.
Highlights required defense-in-depth checks for protected routes.
limitations: Does not implement authentication code by itself.
Assumes the referenced project paths and helpers already exist.
Does not validate environment variables or provider credentials.
Does not cover password storage, token rotation, or threat modeling.

use_cases.title: Protect a new API route
use_cases.description: Apply the correct auth wrapper before adding business logic to a sensitive endpoint.

use_cases.title: Fetch user data in the UI
use_cases.description: Use the approved frontend hook instead of unsupported session access patterns.

use_cases.title: Review auth architecture
use_cases.description: Locate the key auth files and verify that middleware is backed by route-level checks.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Route Protection
prompt_templates.prompt: I am adding a new API route. Tell me which auth wrapper this project expects and what checks I should include.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Frontend User Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component's user data access and align it with the approved auth-handler guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Server Auth Checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design server-side auth checks for this feature using the project auth import and user plan lookup.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Defense in Depth
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these auth-related routes for missing middleware assumptions, route wrappers, and admin checks.

output_examples.input: Which protection should I use for a normal private API route?
output_examples.output: Use the standard authenticated route wrapper. Keep middleware enabled, but add route-level protection before handling user data.

output_examples.input: How should a client component read the current user?
output_examples.output: Use the project user hook. Avoid direct session hooks that the skill marks as unsupported for this application.

output_examples.input: Where should I look when debugging login behavior?
output_examples.output: Start with the auth configuration, then review middleware and the auth helper files listed in the reference.

best_practices: Protect every sensitive route with the correct wrapper, even when middleware already checks access.
Use project-approved user access helpers consistently across client and server code.
Review environment variables and provider configuration before changing login behavior.
anti_patterns: Relying only on middleware for API authorization.
Using unsupported session hooks in client components.
Assuming minimal session data contains plan or role details.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It only provides markdown guidance and references project files.

faq.question: Which framework does it target?
faq.answer: It targets a NextAuth v5 application with custom route wrappers and user helpers.

faq.question: Can it create a full authentication system?
faq.answer: No. It guides changes within an existing project authentication architecture.

faq.question: Does it replace security review?
faq.answer: No. Use it for project conventions, then review authorization logic and sensitive data handling.

faq.question: Why does it mention defense in depth?
faq.answer: Middleware can be bypassed by mistakes or new routes, so each route should enforce its own checks.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
